title: techmap: incorrect lowering of $shift/$shiftx cells (off-by-who-knows-what in width calculations)
body:
```
$ cat t2.v
module top(A, B, Y);
  input [8:0] A;
  input [1:0] B;
  output [2:0] Y;
  $shiftx #(.A_WIDTH(9), .B_WIDTH(4), .Y_WIDTH(3), .A_SIGNED(1'b0), .B_SIGNED(1'b0)) cel (.A(A), .B({B, 2'h0}), .Y(Y));
endmodule
$ yosys t2.v -p "eval -set A 9'h100 -set B 2; techmap; eval -set A 9'h100 -set B 2" -f 'verilog -icells'
[...]

2. Executing EVAL pass (evaluate the circuit given an input).
Eval result: \Y = 3'xx1.

3. Executing TECHMAP pass (map to technology primitives).

[...]
4. Executing EVAL pass (evaluate the circuit given an input).
Eval result: \Y = 3'xxx.
```
